data.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with this child (S6 Docs)]

Audited this Docs child's contribution to the parent's acceptance/success criteria, with each absence cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (S1 6d56, S2 70c7, S3 fa49, S4 30b3, S5 b7fa). This child contributes the documentation slice and the union of children covers every parent criterion. No uncovered parent criterion. Empty findings list.

4-bucket coverage map (parent AC -> covering child/children; this child's role called out):
- Parent AC1 (no attested gate reads working tree; all read snapshot at pinned SHA) — FULLY covered by S1 (materialize core) + S3 (gate re-rooting). This child contributes the ref/source-semantics docs (its AC3) describing the attested-reads-snapshot behavior. Not an absence.
- Parent AC2 (signed verdict records verified_at_sha + reproducible) — FULLY covered by S4 (record+sign) + S1 (immutable SHA). This child documents the HMAC trust model + in-toto Statement shape (its AC4), which S4's own AC5 explicitly delegates here ("Docs state the HMAC trust model + limits (delivered via S6)"). Covered-by-named-consumer; clean.
- Parent AC3 (local mode reads in-place checkout, cannot sign) — FULLY covered by S3 (local in-place read) + S4 (signing bar). This child documents the local-vs-attested + fail-closed-vs-local-still-runs semantics (its AC2/AC3). Not an absence.
- Parent AC4 (concurrent gates, no lock contention/corruption) — FULLY covered by S1 + S2. This child has no doc obligation here and claims none; STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child (a pure-implementation criterion), covered elsewhere. Not an absence.
- Parent AC5 (cache + janitor reclaim under pressure) — FULLY covered by S2. This child documents the disk-cap behavior (its AC5). Not an absence.
- Parent AC6 (private-repo fetch failure surfaces descriptive actionable credential error) — FULLY covered: S1 raises the error; this child documents the requirement + the message + remedy + fail-closed/local-still-runs (its AC1/AC2). This child is the primary doc consumer; clean.
- Parent AC7 (portable: no external tar, no hardcoded /tmp, flock+mkdir fallback) — FULLY covered by S1 (implementation). This child documents REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR + EFS/NFS flock caveat (its AC5). Not an absence.
- Parent AC8 (all six child stories closed; make lint + full test suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion criterion; covered by the existence of all six children (this child is story #6 of 6 in the roster) + each child's own green-bar obligation. Not an absence at this child.

Success-criteria narrative (every gate verifies a client-specified immutable reproducible snapshot; signed verdicts reproducible + bound to verified_at_sha; unsigned local preserves verify-before-push) decomposes onto AC1-AC3 above; this child's documentation of ref/source semantics + HMAC trust model + credential requirement is the operator-facing surface of that narrative and adds no uncovered obligation.

Conclusion: this child cleanly covers the docs slice of the parent criteria; no parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered by the roster. No G3 finding for this pairing.
